VAL-W8A8-002.evidence: test_w8a8_gemm.py: ALL TESTS PASSED. 128x4096x4096: max_abs=0.00781 max_rel=0.05% PASS. 4096x4096x4096: max_abs=0.01548 max_rel=0.05% PASS. 11 test cases including tile boundary (M=65,N=65), non-aligned M (7x64x128), GEMV via GEMM (M=1), K alignment (32/48/64). Unit scale exactness: max_abs=0.0000. Per-channel scale applied correctly. Performance: 128x4096x4096=3.4 TFLOPS, 4096x4096x4096=12.1 TFLOPS.

nonBlockingIssues.description: W8A8 GEMM performance at 12.1 TFLOPS (45% of peak 26.8 TFLOPS FP16, or ~24% of peak INT8 TOPS on gfx906) is not exceptional. The LDS staging with TILE_K=16 INT8 elements per iteration may be too small, leading to high LDS bandwidth pressure relative to compute.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Could explore TILE_K=32 (32 INT8 elements = 8 int32 per thread per K-step). Larger tile sizes reduce loop overhead and may improve occupancy utilization. Non-blocking since correctness is proven.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The kernel K must be a multiple of TILE_K=16. This is satisfied for all Qwen shapes (K=4096 is divisible by 16) but is not checked at runtime. A K value like K=6 or K=10 would produce incorrect results due to out-of-bounds LDS loads.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add a runtime assertion: assert K % 16 == 0, or handle non-multiples with boundary masking. For production deployment with fixed Qwen shapes this is low priority.

sharedStateObservations.observation: W8A8 GEMM tile layout: 64x64 output tiles, TILE_K=16, 256 threads (16x16 per-thread 4x4 outputs). Uses LDS for A[64x16]+B[64x16] tiles = 2048 bytes LDS. K must be multiple of TILE_K=16. Performance 3.4-12.1 TFLOPS for Qwen prefill shapes.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedContent: Document in architecture.md: W8A8 GEMM requires K % 16 == 0 (satisfied for all Qwen shapes). Tiling: M_TILE=N_TILE=64, K_TILE=16. Performance dominated by LDS bandwidth for small M (prefill token-by-token). For large M batch prefill, W8A8 GEMM may exceed W4A16 GEMM despite 2x weight bytes due to INT32 accumulation precision.

summary: Feature fully satisfies VAL-W8A8-002. W8A8 tiled GEMM uses v_dot4_i32_i8 inner loop, INT32 accumulators, FP32 scale epilogue, and handles all representative Qwen shapes and tile boundary conditions. Correctness verified for 11 shapes including edge cases. No blocking issues.
